sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_data (id INT, example_string VARCHAR, example_timestamp TIMESTAMP);

SELECT id, length_grapheme(example_string) AS grapheme_count, example_timestamp - INTERVAL length_grapheme(example_string) DAY AS modified_timestamp FROM test_data WHERE modified_timestamp IS NOT NULL;

SELECT A.id, A.example_string, length_grapheme(A.example_string) AS grapheme_count, B.modified_timestamp FROM test_data A JOIN (SELECT example_timestamp - INTERVAL 5 DAY AS modified_timestamp FROM test_data WHERE example_string IS NOT NULL) B ON A.example_timestamp = B.modified_timestamp;
SELECT example_string, COUNT(*) FROM test_data GROUP BY example_string HAVING length_grapheme(example_string) > 0;
SELECT id, length_grapheme(example_string) + 1 AS increased_grapheme_count FROM test_data WHERE example_string IS NOT NULL;
SELECT (SELECT length_grapheme(example_string) FROM test_data WHERE id = 1) - (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_data WHERE example_string IS NOT NULL);
CREATE TABLE more_test_data (value TEXT, time_record TIMESTAMP);
INSERT INTO more_test_data VALUES ('🙌', TIMESTAMP '2023-10-05 09:00:00'), ('', NULL), (NULL, TIMESTAMP '2023-10-07 10:20:00');
SELECT value, length_grapheme(value) AS grapheme_count FROM more_test_data WHERE length_grapheme(value) IS NOT NULL;

SELECT A.value, A.time_record, length_grapheme(A.value) - IFNULL(B.length, 0) AS final_length FROM more_test_data A LEFT JOIN (SELECT value, length_grapheme(value) AS length FROM more_test_data WHERE value IS NOT NULL) B ON A.value = B.value;
